name: Power Co-Op Employees
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union serving employees of Corn Belt Power Co-op and 40+ select employer groups, offering savings, CDs, mortgages, and loans from Humboldt, Iowa.
description_long:
Power Co-op Employees Credit Union was established in 1956 and operates as a member-owned financial cooperative headquartered in Humboldt, Iowa. With approximately $75.9 million in assets, $64.53 million in deposits, and $53.22 million in loans outstanding, it serves a specialized membership base primarily composed of employees and members of Corn Belt Power Co-op and its affiliated cooperatives, plus employees from over 40 select employer groups including Boone Valley Electric Cooperative, Calhoun County ECA, and Iowa Lakes Electric Coop.

The credit union offers a full suite of banking products including savings accounts, CDs (1-year, 2-year, and 5-year terms), mortgages (both 30-year and 15-year fixed rates), home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, personal loans, and checking accounts. As of April 2026, notable rates include 4.20% APY on savings, 4.59% on 1-year CDs, and 4.91% on 30-year mortgages, positioning it competitively within the credit union space.

What distinguishes Power Co-Op Employees is its restricted field of membership model—access is limited to employees and members of specific cooperatives and affiliated organizations rather than serving the general public. This membership structure allows the credit union to serve a tight-knit community of agricultural and electric cooperative employees with products tailored to their financial needs and circumstances. The organization maintains full NCUA insurance protection and is state-chartered under charter number 61920.

As a smaller regional credit union, Power Co-Op Employees delivers stable financial services to its defined membership community. However, the restricted membership eligibility and limited online rate transparency present constraints for prospective members seeking comparison shopping or those outside the qualifying employer groups. The credit union's smaller asset base ($75.9M) means it may have fewer digital banking features compared to larger national credit unions, though membership qualification typically ensures personalized service. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Member financial services tailored to cooperative employees
pros: NCUA-insured deposits protect member funds up to $250,000
Competitive CD rates (4.59% on 1-year CDs as of April 2026)
Full range of loan products including mortgages, HELOCs, auto, and personal loans
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns incentives with member interests
Long-established institution operating since 1956 with stable financial footing
Specialized service model tailored to agricultural and electric cooperative employee communities
Reasonable mortgage rates (4.91% on 30-year fixed as of April 2026)
cons: Restricted membership—only available to employees/members of qualifying cooperatives and 40+ select employer groups; not open to general public
Limited online rate transparency; website does not display current deposit and loan rates for most product categories
Smaller asset base ($75.9M) may result in fewer digital banking features and services compared to larger credit unions
No information available regarding mobile banking, online account opening, or digital service capabilities
Geographic limitation as a single-branch operation based in rural Iowa limits physical accessibility
best_for: Employees and members of Corn Belt Power Co-op and its affiliated electric cooperatives
Members of the 40+ select employer groups served by the credit union
Rural agricultural and utility industry workers seeking community-focused banking services
Consumers prioritizing member-owned institutional alignment over large national banking chains
diagnosis: Power Co-Op Employees Credit Union is best for employees and members of Corn Belt Power Co-op and its 40+ affiliated employer groups seeking stable, member-owned banking with competitive rates and a full loan suite. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—this institution is not accessible to the general public and serves only a defined cooperative employee community.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
